title: EU to decide on steel safeguard extension
bodyHtml:
As known, the European Union has been implementing protective measures against the import of certain steel products, and this measure has been extended until June 30, 2024, by the Commission decision dated June 25, 2021.

In a letter received from the Ministry of Trade, it is stated that a final review investigation has been initiated, according to the notification published in the Official Journal of the European Union dated February 9, 2024, available at the link below. Within the scope of this review, the Commission will examine the current quota utilization rates, changes in the usage patterns of these quotas, potential exclusionary effects caused by the current quota regime, the evaluation procedure of unused quotas in the last quarter of the quota period, and whether any adjustments due to changing conditions are necessary for the interests of the Union.

The continuation of the letter mentions that the list of developing countries exempted from the measure by the Commission will be updated according to current conditions. Additionally, it is mentioned that relevant parties who wish to bring up additional issues in the investigation can submit their written opinions, provide sufficient evidence supporting their claims, and propose how any developments affecting a product category will be addressed.

Furthermore, the notification states that interested parties can submit their written opinions until February 26, 2024, and all written applications must be made through the https://tron.trade.ec.europa.eu/tron/TDI address.

https://policy.trade.ec.europa.eu/news/eu-decide-steel-safeguard-extension-2024-02-09_en

Notice of Initiation
